MidribV3.TokenVolume[]","name":"_volumes","type":"tuple[]"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"_nonce","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"_signature","type":"bytes"}],"name":"settleBatch","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"_token","type":"address"}],"name":"settleFees","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"holder","type":"address"},{"internalType":"address","name":"token","type":"address"}],"name":"tradeBalance","outputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"amount","type":"uint256"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"nonce","type":"uint256"}],"name":"usedSettleNonces","outputs":[{"internalType":"bool","name":"used","type":"bool"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"account","type":"address"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"nonce","type":"uint256"}],"name":"usedWithdrawNonces","outputs":[{"internalType":"bool","name":"used","type":"bool"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"version","outputs":[{"internalType":"uint8","name":"_version","type":"uint8"}],"stateMutability":"pure","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"components":[{"internalType":"address","name":"account","type":"address"},{"internalType":"address","name":"token","type":"address"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"amount","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"nonce","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"expiry","type":"uint256"}],"internalType":"struct MidribV3.WithdrawalVoucher","name":"_v","type":"tuple"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"_signature","type":"bytes"}],"name":"withdraw","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"token","type":"address"}],"name":"withdrawEpochCap","outputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"cap","type":"uint256"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"token","type":"address"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"epoch","type":"uint256"}],"name":"withdrawnInEpoch","outputs":[{"internalType":"uint256","name":"amount","type":"uint256"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"author":"Aspens Technical Team","details":"MidribV3 is the post-refactor successor to the legacy on-chain-order      contract (MidribV2, now retired from the build \u2014 see git history). Under      the Track A optimistic ledger, trading happens off-chain inside the TEE:      orders never touch the chain. The contract only ever sees these fund      movements \u2014 deposits, TEE-signed net settlement (`settleBatch`), TEE-signed      voucher withdrawals (`withdraw`), and fee settlement. All of the legacy      on-chain order machinery (open / openFor / fill / lock / cancel / resolve,      the ERC-7683 origin- and destination-settler conformance, and Permit2) is      gone.Immutable-args layout (packed, set by {MidribFactory.createInstance}),      by byte offset: owner/signer at 0 (20), factory at 20 (20),      maintenanceAddress at 40 (20), maintenanceBps at 60 (uint16, 2),      operatorAdmin at 62 (20). The maintenance fee (Aspens-controlled) is baked      in immutably for this instance; the operator fee (stack-controlled) lives      in mutable storage, set by `operatorAdmin`.Token compatibility assumptions:      This contract is only safe to use against ERC-20 tokens that satisfy ALL      of the following. Markets must be configured against compliant tokens;      the contract does NOT detect or refuse non-compliant ones.        - Standard `transfer` / `transferFrom` semantics: the sender's balance          drops by exactly the transfer amount, with no re-entrant or          opportunistically-reverting transfer hooks.        - No fee-on-transfer behavior. The `balanceBefore` / `balanceAfter`          reconciliation in `_deposit` assumes the received amount equals the          transferred amount.        - No rebasing / share-based balance model. Balances must be stable          between reads.        - No transfer blocklists that can lock a user's funds after deposit.      Token gating happens off-chain at the arborter / matching-engine      market-config layer. See the SDK README \"Token Assumptions\" section and      the admin-console Add-Token tooltip.The chain's NATIVE asset (ETH/FLR) is additionally supported as a      first-class balance under the `NATIVE` sentinel key: deposited via      `depositNative`/`depositNativeFor` (payable), withdrawn by the same      TEE voucher flow (paid out as raw value), and settled/fee-skimmed by      `settleBatch`/`settleFees` exactly like any token address.","errors":{"FEE_TOO_HIGH(uint256)":[{"details":"A fee (maintenance alone, or maintenance + operator combined)      exceeded DENOMINATOR (100%). `totalBps` is the rejected total."}],"INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE(uint256,uint160)":[{"details":"A balance is insufficient for the requested movement. `available` is      the balance on hand; `required` the amount the caller asked for."}],"INVALID_SIGNER()":[{"details":"A TEE-signed payload (settleBatch / withdrawal voucher) did not      recover to the instance owner (the TEE)."}],"MINIMUM_ORDER_AMOUNT(uint160)":[{"details":"A deposit was below `MIN_ORDER_SIZE` (the dust guard). `amount` is the      rejected value."}],"Reentrancy()":[{"details":"Unauthorized reentrant call."}],"SETTLE_NONCE_ALREADY_USED()":[{"details":"A `settleBatch` nonce was already consumed (replay)."}],"SETTLE_NOT_CONSERVED()":[{"details":"A `settleBatch`'s per-token net deltas did not sum to exactly the      negated contract-computed fee (would create or destroy value)."}],"UNAUTHORIZED_CALLER()":[{"details":"An owner/admin-gated instance function was called by a non-owner."}],"WITHDRAW_NONCE_ALREADY_USED()":[{"details":"A withdrawal voucher's `(account, nonce)` pair was already consumed (replay)."}],"WITHDRAW_RATE_LIMITED(uint256,uint256)":[{"details":"A withdrawal would exceed the per-token per-epoch cap (a TEE-bug      blast-radius backstop). `attempted` is the resulting cumulative epoch total."}],"WITHDRAW_VOUCHER_EXPIRED()":[{"details":"A withdrawal voucher was submitted after its `expiry`."}],"ZERO_ADDRESS_OPERATOR()":[{"details":"A non-zero operator fee was set with a zero recipient."}],"ZERO_ADDRESS_OPERATOR_ADMIN()":[{"details":"A zero address was supplied for the operator admin."}],"ZERO_AMOUNT()":[{"details":"A deposit or withdrawal amount was zero."}]},"events":{"FeesSettled(address,address,uint256,address,uint256)":{"details":"Emitted by `settleFees`: accrued maintenance + operator fees for a token      paid out to their respective recipients' `tradeBalance`."},"NetSettled(address,address,int256)":{"details":"Emitted per account in a `settleBatch`: net change applied to      `tradeBalance[user][tokenContract]` (off-chain shadow-ledger net      settlement, Track A \u00a71c)."},"OperatorAdminSet(address,address)":{"details":"Emitted when an instance's operator admin (the stack-admin key gating the operator fee) is rotated."},"OperatorFeeSet(address,uint16)":{"details":"Emitted when an instance's operator fee (stack-admin-controlled) is set or rotated."},"TokenDeposited(address,address,uint160)":{"details":"Emitted when a token is deposited into the Midrib contract"},"Withdrawn(address,address,uint256,uint256)":{"details":"Emitted on a TEE-voucher-authorized withdrawal (Track A \u00a78). Carries      the voucher `nonce` so the off-chain listener fold is idempotent on      `(account, nonce)`."}},"kind":"dev","methods":{"deposit(address,uint160)":{"params":{"_amount":"amount of tokens we want to deposit","_token":"contract address of the token we are depositing"}},"depositFor(address,address,uint160)":{"details":"Enables the Flare FCE on-chain deposit path (see      infra/stacks/fcc/extension/contracts/AspensInstructionSender.sol): the relayer      holds the user's tokens transiently, approves this contract, and calls      `depositFor` so custody AND the credited tradeBalance both land here \u2014 keeping      `settleBatch`/`withdraw` unchanged \u2014 while the cosigned instruction names the      real depositor. Unlike `deposit`, tokens are pulled from `msg.sender` (the      relayer), not from `_account`; the credit + event are against `_account`. Same      fee-on-transfer-safe accounting as `_deposit`; `_deposit` itself is untouched.","params":{"_account":"beneficiary whose tradeBalance is credited","_amount":"amount to pull from msg.sender (>= MIN_ORDER_SIZE)","_token":"token being deposited"}},"depositNativeFor(address)":{"params":{"_account":"beneficiary whose NATIVE tradeBalance is credited"}},"eip712Domain()":{"details":"See: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-5267"},"factory()":{"returns":{"_factory":"the deploying factory address"}},"multicall(bytes[])":{"details":"Apply `delegatecall` with the current contract to each calldata in `data`, and store the `abi.encode` formatted results of each `delegatecall` into `results`. If any of the `delegatecall`s reverts, the entire context is reverted, and the error is bubbled up. By default, this function directly returns the results and terminates the call context. If you need to add before and after actions to the multicall, please override this function."},"owner()":{"returns":{"_owner":"the instance owner / TEE signer address"}},"setOperatorAdmin(address)":{"params":{"_newAdmin":"new operator admin (non-zero)"}},"setOperatorFee(address,uint16)":{"params":{"_bps":"operator fee in basis points","_recipient":"operator fee recipient (required when `_bps > 0`)"}},"setWithdrawEpochCap(address,uint256)":{"params":{"_cap":"cumulative amount withdrawable per `WITHDRAW_EPOCH` (`0` = unlimited)","_token":"token whose cap to set"}},"settleBatch((address,address,int256)[],(address,uint256)[],uint256,bytes)":{"details":"Off-chain shadow-ledger net settlement (Track A \u00a71c). In the optimistic      model funds are NOT locked per order; matched trades accumulate as net      deltas off-chain and are reflected on-chain here in a single batch.      Safety:      - signed by the instance owner (the TEE);      - per token, the contract computes        `fee = grossVolume*maintenanceBps/DENOMINATOR (floor) + grossVolume*operatorBps/DENOMINATOR (floor)`        and requires the trader deltas to sum to exactly `-fee` \u2014 the fee is the        conservation sink. Non-bypassable: under- OR over-paying the computed        fee reverts SETTLE_NOT_CONSERVED, so the on-chain bps is enforced        against the reported volume;      - the skim accrues to `maintenanceAccrued`/`operatorAccrued`, paid out        later (slower cadence) by `settleFees`;      - no account's `tradeBalance` may go negative (no overdraw / mint);      - one-shot per `_nonce` (replay-protected).","params":{"_deltas":"per-(user,token) signed NET-OF-FEE balance changes","_nonce":"unique batch nonce","_signature":"owner EIP-712 signature over (deltasHash, volumesHash, nonce)","_volumes":"per-token gross matched volume (drives the fee computation)"}},"settleFees(address)":{"params":{"_token":"token whose accrued fees to pay out"}},"version()":{"returns":{"_version":"The version number"}},"withdraw((address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256),bytes)":{"details":"In the optimistic shadow-ledger model a self-service withdrawal is      unsafe: on-chain `tradeBalance` still includes funds reserved behind      resting orders (reservations are off-chain), so a user could drain      collateral a later `settleBatch` is obligated to pay out. The TEE \u2014      the authority on the withdrawable balance \u2014 signs a voucher instead;      the contract enforces only bounded backstops:      - signed by the instance owner (the TEE), like `settleBatch`;      - not past `expiry`; one-shot per `(account, nonce)` (replay-proof);      - hard cap: never pays more of a token than `tradeBalance` holds (no mint);      - per-token per-epoch rate limit (TEE-bug blast-radius backstop).      Anyone may submit it (gas payer need not be the recipient): funds      always go to `_v.account`, so a relayer can pay gas for a gasless      withdrawal.","params":{"_signature":"owner EIP-712 signature over the voucher fields","_v":"the signed voucher"}}},"stateVariables":{"DENOMINATOR":{"details":"Basis-point denominator (100% = 10_000). Matches MidribFactory + the Solana program."},"MIN_ORDER_SIZE":{"details":"Minimum deposit amount \u2014 a dust guard inherited from V2."},"NATIVE":{"details":"Sentinel \"token address\" keying the chain's NATIVE asset (ETH/FLR)      in `tradeBalance`, vouchers, `settleBatch` deltas/volumes, and fee      accrual. Purely a mapping key \u2014 no code lives at it and no ERC-20      call is ever made against it. Native value can ONLY enter via      `depositNative`/`depositNativeFor` (there is no `receive()`), so      `address(this).balance` always equals the sum of sentinel-keyed      trade balances plus sentinel-keyed accrued fees."},"VERSION":{"details":"Current version number"},"WITHDRAW_EPOCH":{"details":"Window for the per-token withdrawal rate-limit accounting (Track A \u00a78)."},"maintenanceAccrued":{"details":"Per-token fees skimmed in `settleBatch` but not yet paid out. Kept in two      pools so a later operator bps/recipient change can't mis-attribute fees      already accrued at the old rate. Drained by `settleFees`."},"operatorAddress":{"details":"Operator fee (stack-controlled) recipient + rate, in basis points. Mutable;      set/rotated by `operatorAdmin` via `setOperatorFee`. Skimmed in ADDITION to      the immutable maintenance fee. Zero until configured."},"operatorAdminOverride":{"details":"Optional override of the baked-in operator admin (immutable arg at byte 62), letting      the stack admin rotate the key that gates the operator fee. `0` = use the baked      value. Read the effective admin via `operatorAdmin()`."},"tradeBalance":{"details":"settled on-chain balance: holder => token => amount"},"usedSettleNonces":{"details":"Nonces already consumed by `settleBatch` (TEE-signed net settlement)."},"usedWithdrawNonces":{"details":"`(account, nonce)` pairs already consumed by a withdrawal voucher      (Track A \u00a78). Per-account so users' voucher nonces are independent."},"withdrawEpochCap":{"details":"Per-token cumulative withdrawal cap per epoch \u2014 a TEE-bug      blast-radius backstop. `0` (the default) means unlimited; the      instance owner raises caps from a conservative start as confidence      grows (`setWithdrawEpochCap`)."},"withdrawnInEpoch":{"details":"Cumulative amount withdrawn per `(token, epoch)`, where      `epoch = block.timestamp / WITHDRAW_EPOCH`."}},"title":"Midrib V3","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"deposit(address,uint160)":{"notice":"Allows a user to deposit tokens into this contract."},"depositFor(address,address,uint160)":{"notice":"Deposit on behalf of `_account`, pulling the tokens from the CALLER."},"depositNative()":{"notice":"Deposit the chain's native asset (ETH/FLR), credited under the         NATIVE sentinel key. The native analogue of `deposit`."},"depositNativeFor(address)":{"notice":"Native-asset analogue of `depositFor` (the Flare FCE relayer         path): the VALUE comes from the caller, while the credit + event         go to `_account`."},"factory()":{"notice":"The factory that deployed this instance (immutable arg at byte 20)."},"maintenanceAddress()":{"notice":"Maintenance fee recipient \u2014 Aspens-controlled, immutable for this instance."},"maintenanceBps()":{"notice":"Maintenance fee in basis points \u2014 Aspens-controlled, immutable for this instance."},"operatorAdmin()":{"notice":"Effective operator admin: the storage override if rotated, else the baked-in         immutable arg (byte 62). Gates `setOperatorFee` / `setOperatorAdmin`."},"owner()":{"notice":"The instance owner \u2014 the TEE signer (immutable arg at byte 0).         Authorizes `settleBatch` and withdrawal vouchers, and gates         `setWithdrawEpochCap`."},"setOperatorAdmin(address)":{"notice":"Rotate the operator admin (current operator admin only)."},"setOperatorFee(address,uint16)":{"notice":"Set or rotate the operator fee (stack admin only). Skimmed IN ADDITION to         the maintenance fee; the combined fee may not exceed DENOMINATOR (100%).         A zero `_bps` disables the operator fee."},"setWithdrawEpochCap(address,uint256)":{"notice":"Set the per-epoch withdrawal cap for a token (instance owner only).         `0` disables the cap (unlimited). Caps bound the blast radius of a         TEE bug; start conservative and raise as confidence grows."},"settleBatch((address,address,int256)[],(address,uint256)[],uint256,bytes)":{"notice":"Apply a TEE-signed batch of net balance changes to `tradeBalance`,         skimming the maintenance + operator fees the contract computes from         the reported per-token gross matched volume."},"settleFees(address)":{"notice":"Pay out the accrued maintenance + operator fees for a token to their         configured recipients' `tradeBalance` (from which they withdraw by         voucher). Intended to run on a slower cadence than `settleBatch`.         Permissionless \u2014 it only ever moves accrued fees to the on-chain         configured addresses."},"version()":{"notice":"Get current version of the contract"},"withdraw((address,address,uint256,uint256,uint256),bytes)":{"notice":"Withdraw tokens against a TEE-signed voucher (Track A \u00a78)."}},"notice":"Optimistic shadow-ledger settlement USL-1.1","urls":["bzz-raw://b099c0d4dcc8b836de7ab1965d62e8454eebb2b2f631542c61b1a2983ee32d7c","dweb:/ipfs/QmNojXo92kVn8RA484mKjVui79xchSTNFyTSWH2eBzs2JW"]}},"version":1}

params._amount: amount of tokens we want to deposit
params._token: contract address of the token we are depositing

depositFor(address,address,uint160).details: Enables the Flare FCE on-chain deposit path (see      infra/stacks/fcc/extension/contracts/AspensInstructionSender.sol): the relayer      holds the user's tokens transiently, approves this contract, and calls      `depositFor` so custody AND the credited tradeBalance both land here — keeping      `settleBatch`/`withdraw` unchanged — while the cosigned instruction names the      real depositor. Unlike `deposit`, tokens are pulled from `msg.sender` (the      relayer), not from `_account`; the credit + event are against `_account`. Same      fee-on-transfer-safe accounting as `_deposit`; `_deposit` itself is untouched.

params._amount: amount to pull from msg.sender (>= MIN_ORDER_SIZE)

params._account: beneficiary whose NATIVE tradeBalance is credited

multicall(bytes[]).details: Apply `delegatecall` with the current contract to each calldata in `data`, and store the `abi.encode` formatted results of each `delegatecall` into `results`. If any of the `delegatecall`s reverts, the entire context is reverted, and the error is bubbled up. By default, this function directly returns the results and terminates the call context. If you need to add before and after actions to the multicall, please override this function.

returns._owner: the instance owner / TEE signer address

settleBatch((address,address,int256)[],(.details: Off-chain shadow-ledger net settlement (Track A §1c). In the optimistic      model funds are NOT locked per order; matched trades accumulate as net      deltas off-chain and are reflected on-chain here in a single batch.      Safety:      - signed by the instance owner (the TEE);      - per token, the contract computes        `fee = grossVolume*maintenanceBps/DENOMINATOR (floor) + grossVolume*operatorBps/DENOMINATOR (floor)`        and requires the trader deltas to sum to exactly `-fee` — the fee is the        conservation sink. Non-bypassable: under- OR over-paying the computed        fee reverts SETTLE_NOT_CONSERVED, so the on-chain bps is enforced        against the reported volume;      - the skim accrues to `maintenanceAccrued`/`operatorAccrued`, paid out        later (slower cadence) by `settleFees`;      - no account's `tradeBalance` may go negative (no overdraw / mint);      - one-shot per `_nonce` (replay-protected).

params._signature: owner EIP-712 signature over (deltasHash, volumesHash, nonce)
params._volumes: per-token gross matched volume (drives the fee computation)

params._token: token whose accrued fees to pay out

withdraw((address,address,uint256,uint25.details: In the optimistic shadow-ledger model a self-service withdrawal is      unsafe: on-chain `tradeBalance` still includes funds reserved behind      resting orders (reservations are off-chain), so a user could drain      collateral a later `settleBatch` is obligated to pay out. The TEE —      the authority on the withdrawable balance — signs a voucher instead;      the contract enforces only bounded backstops:      - signed by the instance owner (the TEE), like `settleBatch`;      - not past `expiry`; one-shot per `(account, nonce)` (replay-proof);      - hard cap: never pays more of a token than `tradeBalance` holds (no mint);      - per-token per-epoch rate limit (TEE-bug blast-radius backstop).      Anyone may submit it (gas payer need not be the recipient): funds      always go to `_v.account`, so a relayer can pay gas for a gasless      withdrawal.

params._signature: owner EIP-712 signature over the voucher fields

deposit(address,uint160).notice: Allows a user to deposit tokens into this contract.

depositFor(address,address,uint160).notice: Deposit on behalf of `_account`, pulling the tokens from the CALLER.

depositNative().notice: Deposit the chain's native asset (ETH/FLR), credited under the         NATIVE sentinel key. The native analogue of `deposit`.

depositNativeFor(address).notice: Native-asset analogue of `depositFor` (the Flare FCE relayer         path): the VALUE comes from the caller, while the credit + event         go to `_account`.

factory().notice: The factory that deployed this instance (immutable arg at byte 20).

maintenanceAddress().notice: Maintenance fee recipient — Aspens-controlled, immutable for this instance.

maintenanceBps().notice: Maintenance fee in basis points — Aspens-controlled, immutable for this instance.

operatorAdmin().notice: Effective operator admin: the storage override if rotated, else the baked-in         immutable arg (byte 62). Gates `setOperatorFee` / `setOperatorAdmin`.

owner().notice: The instance owner — the TEE signer (immutable arg at byte 0).         Authorizes `settleBatch` and withdrawal vouchers, and gates         `setWithdrawEpochCap`.

setOperatorAdmin(address).notice: Rotate the operator admin (current operator admin only).

setOperatorFee(address,uint16).notice: Set or rotate the operator fee (stack admin only). Skimmed IN ADDITION to         the maintenance fee; the combined fee may not exceed DENOMINATOR (100%).         A zero `_bps` disables the operator fee.

setWithdrawEpochCap(address,uint256).notice: Set the per-epoch withdrawal cap for a token (instance owner only).         `0` disables the cap (unlimited). Caps bound the blast radius of a         TEE bug; start conservative and raise as confidence grows.

settleBatch((address,address,int256)[],(.notice: Apply a TEE-signed batch of net balance changes to `tradeBalance`,         skimming the maintenance + operator fees the contract computes from         the reported per-token gross matched volume.

settleFees(address).notice: Pay out the accrued maintenance + operator fees for a token to their         configured recipients' `tradeBalance` (from which they withdraw by         voucher). Intended to run on a slower cadence than `settleBatch`.         Permissionless — it only ever moves accrued fees to the on-chain         configured addresses.

version().notice: Get current version of the contract

withdraw((address,address,uint256,uint25.notice: Withdraw tokens against a TEE-signed voucher (Track A §8).
